title: Plumbing, Heating, and Air-Conditioning Contractors

alternatives.name: HVAC-focused installation and service contractor
inclusion_criteria: The firm is classified in NAICS 238220 and its primary operating identity is heating, ventilation, and air-conditioning installation, replacement, maintenance, or repair.
HVAC work is the firm's largest service line; refrigeration work may be incidental but is not the primary operating identity.
The firm is in the dataset's estimated $1-10M EBITDA band, represented by the 100-499 employee firm class.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude firms whose largest service line is plumbing, fire or lawn sprinklers, stand-alone refrigeration, or steam and pipe fitting.
Exclude integrated mechanical contractors whose operating identity spans HVAC, plumbing, piping, and related mechanical systems without HVAC being the largest service line.
Exclude firms outside the dataset's estimated $1-10M EBITDA band.
alternatives.rationale: The Census 2022 kind-of-business table reports $121.738B of HVAC-contractor activity out of $297.609B total NAICS 238220 receipts, a 40.91% national share. Applying that share to the pinned 1,152-firm band gives 471 firms after rounding. The low and high endpoints apply a predeclared 0.75x-1.25x bridge to allow for revenue-per-firm and service-mix differences between the all-size revenue table and 100-499 employee firms.

alternatives.name: Plumbing-focused installation and service contractor
inclusion_criteria: The firm is classified in NAICS 238220 and its primary operating identity is plumbing installation, replacement, maintenance, or repair.
Plumbing work is the firm's largest service line; heating or cooling work may be incidental but is not the primary operating identity.
The firm is in the dataset's estimated $1-10M EBITDA band, represented by the 100-499 employee firm class.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude firms whose largest service line is HVAC, fire or lawn sprinklers, stand-alone refrigeration, or steam and pipe fitting.
Exclude integrated mechanical contractors whose operating identity spans HVAC, plumbing, piping, and related mechanical systems without plumbing being the largest service line.
Exclude firms outside the dataset's estimated $1-10M EBITDA band.
alternatives.rationale: The Census 2022 kind-of-business table reports $75.078B of plumbing-contractor activity out of $297.609B total NAICS 238220 receipts, a 25.23% national share. Applying that share to the pinned 1,152-firm band gives 291 firms after rounding. The endpoints apply the same predeclared 0.75x-1.25x revenue-to-count bridge used for every enumerated archetype.

alternatives.name: Integrated mechanical contractor
inclusion_criteria: The firm is classified in NAICS 238220 and operates as a multi-discipline mechanical contractor across at least two of HVAC, plumbing, piping, refrigeration, or mechanical service.
No single HVAC- or plumbing-focused identity is sufficient to classify the firm into either focused archetype.
The firm is in the dataset's estimated $1-10M EBITDA band, represented by the 100-499 employee firm class.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude firms clearly identified as HVAC-focused or plumbing-focused under the criteria above.
Exclude single-specialty sprinkler, refrigeration, steam-fitting, or pipe-fitting contractors.
Exclude firms outside the dataset's estimated $1-10M EBITDA band.
alternatives.rationale: The Census 2022 kind-of-business table reports $54.365B of mechanical-contractor activity out of $297.609B total NAICS 238220 receipts, an 18.27% national share. Applying that share to the pinned 1,152-firm band gives 210 firms after rounding. MCAA's description of member firms across heating, air conditioning, refrigeration, plumbing, piping, mechanical systems, and service supports treating integrated mechanical contracting as a distinct operating identity. The endpoints apply the common 0.75x-1.25x revenue-to-count bridge.

residual.name: Specialty and non-core 238220 contractors
residual.rationale: This named residual is the balance after the three enumerated kind-of-business shares and includes building and lawn sprinkler contractors, stand-alone refrigeration contractors, steam and pipe-fitting contractors, other specialty construction activity, and secondary nonconstruction activity. Its base is 1,152 minus the three rounded archetype bases; its endpoints use the same 0.75x-1.25x bridge.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition and index entries for 238220
EC2223KOB: Construction, Value of Business Done for Kind-of-Business
Mechanical Contractors Association of America: About MCAA
Air Conditioning Contractors of America: About ACCA

target_archetype.selection_basis: The three enumerated archetypes cover 972 of 1,152 estimated band firms at base, or 84.375%. Their base counts are obtained by multiplying the pinned band count by the corresponding national 2022 Census kind-of-business revenue share and rounding to whole firms: HVAC 1,152 x 40.91% = 471; plumbing 1,152 x 25.23% = 291; integrated mechanical 1,152 x 18.27% = 210. HVAC-focused-contractor is selected because 471 is the largest base count. Each range applies the same 0.75x-1.25x revenue-to-firm-count bridge. The selected HVAC range of 353-589 overlaps the plumbing range of 218-364, so uncertainty is set mechanically even though the base counts differ by more than 10%.

value_basis.scope: Source-free class-neutral employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost role structure for a 100-499 employee HVAC-focused installation and service contractor, measured against controllable value-added revenue.
value_basis.controllable_value_added_boundary: Recognized revenue less only documented consideration contractually or economically owed to an outside provider for scope the operator does not control as value added. Equipment, materials, labor, warranty responsibility, procurement, coordination, and subcontracted scope remain inside controllable value-added revenue whenever the operator controls delivery, bears performance or inventory risk, or earns a markup. A deducted pass-through cost may not also enter employee cash cost or controllable contractor cash cost.
approved_pass_through_categories: Documented permit, inspection, and statutory filing fees remitted to government authorities and contractually reimbursed at cost
Documented customer-directed equipment or material purchases reimbursed at cost with no operator markup, inventory risk, procurement discretion, or warranty obligation
Documented subcontracted services contractually reimbursed at cost where the outside provider, rather than the operator, controls delivery and performance responsibility for that scope

roles.title: Core HVAC field technicians and installers
roles.rationale: Source-free structural weight for non-supervisory employees and controllable labor contractors directly installing, maintaining, diagnosing, and repairing heating, ventilation, and air-conditioning systems; excludes helpers, fabrication specialists, supervisors, sales, and office staff.

roles.title: Field helpers, fabrication, controls, and other trade specialists
roles.rationale: Source-free structural weight for non-supervisory field support and adjacent craft labor not included in core HVAC technicians, including helpers, sheet-metal or shop fabrication, controls, and incidental refrigeration specialists.

roles.title: Field supervision and project management
roles.rationale: Source-free structural weight for foremen, service managers, installation managers, project managers, and other roles directly accountable for field delivery; excludes executives and general operations management.

roles.title: Sales, estimating, and account management
roles.rationale: Source-free structural weight for estimators, comfort advisers, business development, and account management; excludes dispatch, customer intake, and project management.

roles.title: Dispatch, customer support, and transactional administration
roles.rationale: Source-free structural weight for dispatch, scheduling, customer intake, billing, payroll processing, purchasing administration, and general clerical work; excludes sales and management.

roles.title: Executive, general operations, finance, people, and technology leadership
roles.rationale: Source-free structural weight for owners on market compensation, executives, general operations managers, controllers, people leadership, and internal technology leadership not assigned to another category.

value_basis.rationale: The selected HVAC-focused archetype is operationally coherent enough to pre-freeze a mutually exclusive, exhaustive class-neutral role taxonomy without relying on outcome research. The six source-free weights sum to 1.00 and remain ESTIMATE/NONE assumptions. The normalized dataset's LOW five-digit labor share and broader 23822 occupation mix are not treated as target-specific evidence and do not determine the weights.
caveats: The basis describes only the selected HVAC-focused archetype, not NAICS 238220 as a whole.
The role weights are source-free structural assumptions and must not be represented as observed staffing or payroll shares.
The identifiers distinguish employee cash cost from controllable contractor cash cost but do not supply either amount.
The approved categories define eligibility for investigation, not an automatic deduction; every deducted item still requires target-specific documentation.
The dataset's five-digit labor share and occupation mix may not silently replace or relabel this assumption basis as target-specific evidence.

source_hints.source: Selected-archetype audited or reviewed financial statements, general ledger detail, customer contracts, subcontract agreements, equipment and materials invoices, and revenue-recognition policies
source_hints.why: Needed to establish recognized revenue and document each economically or contractually owed third-party pass-through category without deducting operator-controlled value added.

source_hints.source: Selected-archetype payroll register, removable cash-burden schedules, contractor ledgers, role roster, and reconciliations to controllable value-added revenue
source_hints.why: Needed to measure employee cash cost and controllable contractor cash cost, validate mutually exclusive role weights, and prevent pass-through double counting.

source_hints.source: Selected-archetype operator system logs, workflow records, vendor and change-investment invoices, and documented before-and-after operating cases
source_hints.why: Needed to distinguish task changes from cash-realizable changes and to establish timing and investment for the named archetype.

source_hints.source: Customer contracts, maintenance agreements, bid tabs, renewal cohorts, price books, and matched gross-price histories from HVAC-focused contractors
source_hints.why: Needed to measure commercial retention separately from workflow realization and constant-price demand.

source_hints.source: Closed lower-middle-market HVAC contractor transaction census, broker engagement records, ownership records, and comparable deal-flow datasets
source_hints.why: Needed to estimate the five-year share of selected-archetype firms that become genuinely sale available.

source_hints.source: Process access records, bid histories, diligence outcomes, reference-buyer sourcing records, and prevailing market-clearing transaction evidence
source_hints.why: Needed to estimate the separate conditional share of sale-available firms a nonprivileged reference buyer can source, access, diligence, and win.

source_hints.source: Closed transaction documents and state-matched entry and historical exit comparables for HVAC-focused contractors in the specified band
source_hints.why: Needed to measure paid entry pricing and support a downside exit multiple conditional on one frozen operating state without duplicating demand or pricing effects.

source_hints.source: Longitudinal constant-price customer purchasing, service-contract renewal, equipment-service, warranty, and regulatory-accountability evidence for HVAC-focused operators
source_hints.why: Needed to measure paid operator-controlled value-added service demand separately from price, margin, and implementation.

controllable_value_added: What target-archetype evidence establishes recognized revenue, each eligible documented third-party pass-through category, a positive bounded controllable value-added denominator, employee cash cost, controllable contractor cash cost, and mutually exclusive role weights without pass-through double counting?
operational_realization: For a target-specific role structure, what evidence supports each operational realization and implementation-investment input, its timing, and the conversion from changed work to cash cost?
commercial_retention: What contract, renewal, bid, and pricing evidence supports each annual commercial-retention input for a 100-499 employee HVAC-focused contractor?
sale_availability: What evidence supports the five-year sale-availability share for firms meeting the selected archetype and band criteria?
buyer_access_win: Conditional on sale availability, what distinct evidence supports the nonprivileged reference buyer's share of targets it can source, access, diligence, and win at the prevailing market-clearing price?
valuation_robustness: What closed comparable transactions support paid entry and downside exit multiples for one consistently normalized year-five operating state without importing commercial-retention or survival effects?
operator_survival: What evidence supports the constant-price year-five share of baseline controllable value-added demand that still purchases an accountable service from the selected operator archetype?

special_notes: The selected record is the HVAC-focused-contractor archetype only and must not be described as a score or conclusion for all of NAICS 238220.
Selection uncertainty is mandatory because the supported HVAC and plumbing count ranges overlap; downstream research must preserve one selected record and readiness cannot exceed UNPROVEN.

The archetype counts are ESTIMATE/PROXY values based on all-size kind-of-business revenue shares bridged to the dataset's 100-499 employee band, not directly observed firm counts by operating identity.
The class-neutral value basis is an ASSUMPTION with ESTIMATE/NONE roles and no basis sources; it cannot support readiness above UNPROVEN.
Controllable value-added revenue equals recognized revenue minus documented third-party pass-through; undocumented pass-through may not be deducted.
Any cost deducted from recognized revenue as third-party pass-through is excluded from employee cash cost and controllable contractor cash cost to prevent double counting.
The residual is named and bounded but is not an additional primary record.
